action.text:
Dim xlApp As Excel.Application
Dim xlBook As Excel.Workbook
Dim xlSheet As Excel.Worksheet

Set xlApp = New Excel.Application
xlApp.Visible = True
Set xlBook = xlApp.Workbooks.Add
Set xlSheet = xlBook.Worksheets(1)

xlSheet.Columns(num).NumberFormat = '@' 'To prevent Excel from interpreting the content as numerical data
xlSheet.Cells(row, col).Value2 = CStr(<var with numeric type>) 'Write the content as string

Dim filePath As String
filePath = xlApp.Application.GetSaveAsFilename('', 'Excel Files (*.xlsx), *.xlsx', , 'prompt text')
If filePath <> 'false' Then
	xlBook.SaveAs filePath
End If

On Error Resume Next
If Not xlBook Is Nothing Then xlBookb.Close
If Not xlApp Is Nothing Then xlApp.Quit
Set xlSheet = Nothing
Set xlBook = Nothing
Set xlApp = Nothing
On Error GoTo 0

Dim xlApp As Object
Dim xlBook As Object
Dim xlSheet As Object

PathExcelFile = xlApp.Application.GetOpenFilename('Excel Files (*.xlsx), *.xlsx', , 'Prompt text')
If PathExcelFile = 'False' or PathExcelFile = 'Falsch' Then 'Unvalid path
	xlApp.Quit
	Set xlApp = Nothing
End If

Dim lastRow, lastCol As Long
Dim filePath As String
Set xlApp = CreateObject('Excel.Application')
xlApp.Visible = False
Set xlBook = xlApp.Workbooks.Open(filePath)
Set xlSheet = xlBook.Worksheets(1)
lastRow = xlSheet.Cells(xlSheet.Rows.Count, 1).End(-4162).Row
lastCol = xlSheet.Cells(1, xlSheet.Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column

For r = 2 To lastRow
	CDbl(ws.Cells(r, col).Value)
	CStr(ws.Cells(r, col).Value)
Next r
